9:["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-white dark:bg-stone-900 rounded-2xl p-6 sm:p-8 mt-4 shadow-lg border border-stone-100 dark:border-stone-800 mb-10","children":[["$","h1",null,{"className":"text-2xl sm:text-3xl lg:text-4xl font-bold text-stone-900 dark:text-stone-100 leading-tight mb-5","style":{"fontFamily":"'Playfair Display', Georgia, serif"},"children":"Standing Desk Guide: Do You Need One, and Which to Buy in 2026"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"flex flex-wrap items-center gap-4 text-sm text-stone-400","children":[["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/authors/tom-hadley","className":"flex items-center gap-2 group","children":[["$","img",null,{"src":"https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1500648767791-00dcc994a43e?w=400&h=400&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80","alt":"Tom Hadley","className":"w-8 h-8 rounded-full object-cover object-top"}],["$","div",null,{"children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-stone-700 dark:text-stone-200 text-sm group-hover:text-orange-600 transition-colors","children":"Tom Hadley"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xs text-stone-400","children":"Ergonomics Specialist"}]]}]]}],["$","span",null,{"className":"flex items-center gap-1","children":[["$","$L4",null,{"iconNode":[["circle",{"cx":"12","cy":"12","r":"10","key":"1mglay"}],["path",{"d":"M12 6v6l4 2","key":"mmk7yg"}]],"className":"lucide-clock","size":13}]," ","9 min read"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"February 28, 2026"}]]}]]}]
a:["$","div",null,{"className":"grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-10 pb-16","children":[["$","article",null,{"className":"lg:col-span-2","children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xl text-stone-600 leading-relaxed mb-10 font-medium pl-4 border-l-4 border-orange-500","children":"Standing desks promise health benefits, improved energy, and reduced back pain. Some of those claims are well-founded. Some are marketing. This guide separates them — and recommends the best options at every budget."}],["$","div",null,{"className":"prose","children":[["$","h2","0",{"id":"the-problem-with-sitting-all-day","children":"The Problem With Sitting All Day"}],["$","p","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The research on prolonged sitting is not ambiguous. Extended periods of uninterrupted sitting are associated with increased cardiovascular risk, reduced insulin sensitivity, and musculoskeletal problems — particularly in the lower back and hips."}}],["$","p","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The important nuance: the research does not conclude that standing all day is the solution. It concludes that <strong>movement and variation</strong> are what the body needs. A standing desk is a tool for achieving variation — not a replacement for sitting."}}],["$","h2","3",{"id":"what-the-research-actually-shows","children":"What the Research Actually Shows"}],["$","p","4",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Studies on sit-stand desks consistently find:"}}],["$","ul","5",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Reduced lower back discomfort</strong> after four weeks of regular use"}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Improved energy and mood</strong> during afternoon hours"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>No significant caloric advantage</strong> — standing burns approximately 8 more calories per hour than sitting. The health benefit comes from reduced sedentary behaviour, not from calorie expenditure."}}]]}],["$","p","6",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"What the research does not support: the idea that standing is inherently better than sitting. Standing for extended periods without movement causes its own problems — varicose veins, foot and knee pain, and lower back fatigue."}}],["$","p","7",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The prescription is <strong>alternating between sitting and standing</strong> — typically 15–30 minutes of standing per hour."}}],["$","h2","8",{"id":"electric-vs-manual-vs-fixed","children":"Electric vs. Manual vs. Fixed"}],["$","h3","9",{"id":"electric-height-adjustable-desks","children":"Electric Height-Adjustable Desks"}],["$","p","10",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The gold standard for home office use. A motor-driven frame raises and lowers at the press of a button. Transition from sitting to standing takes 5–10 seconds."}}],["$","p","11",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Pros</strong>: Convenient enough to actually use. Programmable heights. Good stability."}}],["$","p","12",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Cons</strong>: More expensive. Motor can fail over time. Heavier."}}],["$","p","13",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>The transition threshold</strong>: if a desk is not convenient to adjust, you will not adjust it. Research shows manual crank desks are used far less frequently than electric ones. If you are buying a sit-stand desk, buy electric."}}],["$","h3","14",{"id":"manual-crank-desks","children":"Manual (Crank) Desks"}],["$","p","15",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A hand-cranked mechanism adjusts height. Takes 30–60 seconds per transition."}}],"$Ld","$Le","$Lf","$L10","$L11","$L12","$L13","$L14","$L15","$L16","$L17","$L18","$L19","$L1a","$L1b","$L1c","$L1d","$L1e","$L1f","$L20","$L21"]}],"$L22","$L23","$L24"]}],"$L25"]}]
b:["$","script","script-0",{"src":"/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js","async":true}]
c:["$","$L26",null,{"children":["$","$27",null,{"name":"Next.MetadataOutlet","children":"$@28"}]}]
d:["$","p","16",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Pros</strong>: Significantly cheaper. No motor to fail."}}]
e:["$","p","17",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Cons</strong>: Inconvenient enough that most people stop alternating within weeks."}}]
f:["$","h2","18",{"id":"the-best-standing-desks-in-2026","children":"The Best Standing Desks in 2026"}]
10:["$","h3","19",{"id":"budget-flexispot-e7-350","children":"Budget: Flexispot E7 (~£350)"}]
11:["$","p","20",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The best entry-level sit-stand desk. Dual motor, 125 kg capacity, four programmable heights, and a robust frame. The tabletop quality is basic but the mechanism is excellent. Add an aftermarket bamboo or solid wood top for a premium feel."}}]
12:["$","h3","21",{"id":"mid-range-uplift-v2-700","children":"Mid-Range: Uplift V2 (~£700)"}]
13:["$","p","22",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Three-stage legs (allowing a wider height range than most competitors), excellent stability at full height, and an outstanding warranty. Comes with a crossbar for stability — essential for wide desks."}}]
14:["$","h3","23",{"id":"premium-vari-electric-standing-desk-950","children":"Premium: Vari Electric Standing Desk (~£950)"}]
15:["$","p","24",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Exceptionally stable at all heights, extremely fast motor, and available in many size and finish options. The build quality is clearly above the competition."}}]
16:["$","h3","25",{"id":"for-small-spaces-flexispot-comhar-all-in-one-400","children":"For Small Spaces: Flexispot Comhar All-in-One (~£400)"}]
17:["$","p","26",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A single-leg design with a round top and integrated storage drawer. Fits into corners and tight spaces where a rectangular dual-motor desk cannot. Surprisingly stable for a single-column design."}}]
18:["$","h2","27",{"id":"what-to-look-for","children":"What to Look For"}]
19:["$","p","28",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Stability at full height</strong>: Many desks wobble significantly at maximum height. Desks with crossbars and three-stage legs are significantly more stable."}}]
1a:["$","p","29",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Height range</strong>: Standard range is approximately 62–125 cm. Verify the range covers your sitting and standing positions if you are outside the average height range."}}]
1b:["$","p","30",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Weight capacity</strong>: A monitor, monitor arm, laptop, and accessories can weigh 20–30 kg. A desk rated for 50 kg minimum is sufficient."}}]
1c:["$","p","31",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Cable management</strong>: Sit-stand desks create cable management challenges as the height changes. Look for desks with built-in cable trays or grommets."}}]
1d:["$","h2","32",{"id":"making-a-standing-desk-work","children":"Making a Standing Desk Work"}]
1e:["$","p","33",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Use a timer</strong>: Set a reminder every 45–60 minutes to transition. Without a reminder, you will stay in one position all day."}}]
1f:["$","p","34",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Use an anti-fatigue mat</strong>: Standing on a hard floor causes foot and joint fatigue within 20–30 minutes. A good anti-fatigue mat (Topo by Ergodriven, ~£80) extends comfortable standing time significantly."}}]
20:["$","p","35",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Do not start with long standing periods</strong>: New sit-stand desk users commonly overuse the standing position initially. Start with 15 minutes per hour and increase gradually."}}]
21:["$","p","36",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A sit-stand desk is a tool, not a cure. Pair it with a <a href=\"/blog/best-ergonomic-office-chairs-2026\" class=\"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline font-medium\">good ergonomic chair</a> and proper positioning for maximum benefit."}}]
